title: The Basement Where Time Counts Itself

features.name: the infinite counter
features.desc-on-touch: A column of mechanical counters stacked from floor to ceiling, each wheel turning at a different rate. You read one number and it has already changed.

features.name: the boiler's rhythm
features.desc-on-touch: Through the dark, a regular pulse of rust-coloured warmth. The boiler is still counting beats, still pushing heat to rooms where no one sleeps.

features.name: the plantation of clocks
features.desc-on-touch: Rows of identical clock faces, hands moving in cascade. Each one shows a different time, but they're all the same moment, measured differently.

features.name: the room that echoes with counting
features.desc-on-touch: Whispered numbers in the dark. Someone—or something—has been down here counting the seconds of every night this motel has held guests. The voice does not stop.

inscription: beneath the motel where numbers forgot they were time

file.name: BoilerLogs_1987_1993_Incomplete_Cyclic.txt
file.note: Power records from the basement showing identical daily patterns for six years, every shift ending at the same second, every record identical except the dates.

exits.label: back up into the lobby where night is still happening
further down where the count becomes the only language spoken
into the mechanical loam where boilers learned to dream
